_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of encryption algorithms and their implementation.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including their application to system architecture, data minimization, purpose limitation, storage limitation, and consent management design patterns.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-K-006 (RC, cosine: 0.483), DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.480).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.8/10 (raw 0.277). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [algorithms, encryption, implementation] vs WIDAI-only terms [application, architecture, consent, data, default, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.887 (P=0.921, R=0.855). P exceeds R by 0.067 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of encryption algorithms and their implementation." and "Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including thei". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.532 > STS 0.277 (gap +0.255). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.0%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [algorithms, encryption, implementation]. WIDAI-only: [application, architecture, consent, data, default, design].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.255 with low STS 0.277. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
